document.title: Reducer Core Concept Map
document.purpose: Establish the early conceptual terrain surrounding the Reducer Core architecture, including core maps, terminology, authority boundaries, neighboring systems, and dictionary-style entries for major concepts.
author: Lion Kimbro
Wing-Cat (Chat-GPT)

orientation.summary: This document is not intended to rigidly finalize the architecture. Its purpose is to preserve architectural insight, stabilize terminology, establish authority boundaries, and create a conceptual map that can guide future implementations.
important-notes: Different applications may implement these ideas differently.
The purpose is not rigid conformity.
The purpose is architectural coherence and shared understanding.
Maps and dictionary entries are intended to preserve distinctions and relationships.
This document is especially intended to support implementation clarity for both humans and AI coding systems.

core-architecture-map.summary: The four major divisions of the architecture and their primary flows.

emits: semantic EVENTS for the DISCRETE ENGINE

notes: The Continuity Engine is fundamentally continuous and episode-based.
The Discrete Engine is fundamentally punctuated and semantic.
The World Model may be vastly larger than the Discrete Engine state.
Projection is downstream from semantic state and world state.

continuity-vs-discrete-map.summary: Contrast between the Continuity Engine and the Discrete Engine.

responsibilities: maintain GUI consistency with editor/workspace state

continuity-engine.title: Continuity Engine
continuity-engine.concise-meaning: A continuous interaction interpretation system responsible for embodied interaction recognition, coordination, temporal interaction episodes, and semantic event extraction.
why-it-exists: To separate interaction continuity from semantic/editor state.
To prevent GUI interaction logic from contaminating semantic application logic.
To centralize interaction coordination and recognition.
open-questions: How much should coordination law be generalized?
How should camera motion relate to continuity systems?

discrete-engine.title: Discrete Engine
discrete-engine.concise-meaning: A semantic transition engine that processes normalized semantic events, maintains compact editor/workspace state, and coordinates lawful semantic transitions.
why-it-exists: To separate semantic/editor logic from GUI interaction chaos.
To make application logic easier to reason about.
To support lawful semantic history and undo/redo.
open-questions: How much workspace state belongs here?

semantic-event.title: Semantic Event
semantic-event.concise-meaning: A normalized, semantically meaningful discrete event intended for processing by the Discrete Engine.
why-it-exists: To separate semantic meaning from raw interaction mechanics.
To normalize multiple interaction modalities into common semantic transitions.
open-questions: What exactly qualifies as semantically historical?
Should semantic events always be replayable?

world-model.title: World Model
world-model.concise-meaning: A large durable modeled reality system responsible for world storage, revision history, indexing, persistence, and reconstruction.
why-it-exists: To separate enormous durable reality from compact editor/workspace state.
To allow the world to scale independently of the editor state.
open-questions: How should reconstruction failure be represented?
How should world history garbage collection work?

projection.title: Projection
projection.concise-meaning: A manifestation system responsible for aligning visible GUI and graphical presentation with editor state, world state, and immediates.
why-it-exists: To separate rendering and manifestation from semantic/editor logic.
To maintain visible consistency with application state.
open-questions: Should camera state belong partly to projection?
How should retained-mode and immediate-mode rendering interact?

notes: Implementation skeletons are planned but intentionally deferred.
The architecture maps and dictionary should stabilize first.
skeletons.future-entry:
Many applications may omit the Continuity Engine entirely.
In such systems, semantic events are emitted directly from GUI toolkit callbacks, menu systems, keyboard bindings, or similar interaction mechanisms into the Discrete Engine.

The Continuity Engine becomes valuable when interaction continuity, gesture recognition, coordination, temporal interaction episodes, or embodied interaction complexity become substantial architectural concerns.

notes: Case studies are planned but intentionally deferred.
Likely future examples include editor systems and merchant databases.

closing-notes.summary: This document preserves the current architectural terrain without attempting premature finalization. The architecture is expected to continue evolving through implementation experience.
